sourceMovie.title: Maurice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Maurice works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pride & Prejudice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maurice clicked for you, Pride & Prejudice (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Maurice?
faq.answer: Try Pride & Prejudice, One Day, Endless Love—each shares DNA with Maurice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Maurice?
faq.answer: Maurice (1987) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Maurice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
